metadata.disclaimer: No feelings were spared in the making of this report

fileSize.verdict: CHONKY BEAST - This thing is 23.8KB of pure complexity

tokenConsumption.verdict: TOKEN HEMORRHAGING - Your wallet is crying

executionTime.totalUserTime: Potentially INFINITE if user enjoys clicking
executionTime.verdict: TIME VAMPIRE - Sucks productivity like Dracula sucks blood

successRateAnalysis.verdict: SCHRÖDINGER'S COMMAND - Simultaneously works and doesn't work until observed

antiPatternViolations.simplicity: VIOLATED - This is the opposite of simple

realWorldUsability.learnability: NIGHTMARE - Requires PhD in complexity theory
realWorldUsability.debuggability: IMPOSSIBLE - Good luck finding bugs in this labyrinth

costBenefitRatio.roi: NEGATIVE - Time investment exceeds any possible benefit

savageBrutaIJudgment.professionalAssessment: This command is what happens when a brilliant mind meets a deadline and decides that MORE IS ALWAYS BETTER. It's like watching someone build a space shuttle to go to the grocery store.
specificCrimes: COMPLEXITY CRIME: 31.2x over CLAUDE.md limits - This isn't engineering, it's performance art
TOKEN TERRORISM: Will drain your Claude budget faster than a leaky dam
TIME THEFT: Interactive loops with no guaranteed exit - User may die of old age
ABSTRACTION ABUSE: JavaScript pseudo-code in Markdown pretending to be executable
CONFIDENCE SCAM: Claims 95% confidence but delivers 0% functional code
evidenceBacked: 672 lines of theoretical complexity with ZERO runnable code
156 complexity points when maximum allowed is 5
Estimated $0.54 per execution for glorified Q&A session
3 while loops that could run forever if user is indecisive
No error handling, testing, or validation whatsoever
savageBrutaIJudgment.scientificConclusion: This command violates every principle of simplicity, efficiency, and sanity known to software engineering. It's a beautiful disaster - like watching a Lamborghini crash in slow motion.

emergencyTreatment: Remove all JavaScript - this is supposed to be a command template
Add actual error handling and exit conditions
therapeuticApproach: Study the simplicity principles in CLAUDE.md like holy scripture
Practice the 3-Question Rule before writing ANY code
Remember: If it needs 672 lines, you're doing it wrong
Get a code review from someone who values their sanity
improvements.medicinalDosage: One dose of humility, taken with a large glass of 'keep it simple, stupid'

verdict.finalWords: This command is proof that just because you CAN build something doesn't mean you SHOULD. It's a masterclass in how to turn a simple interactive prompt into a NASA mission control center.
